title: GM is betting on battery cells that don’t use lithium
summary:
GM has partnered with Peak Energy to develop next-generation sodium-ion battery cells, but they’re not going into EVs – they’re for grid-scale energy storage projects. GM Ventures is making a strategic investment in Peak Energy.

more…

title: BYD is bringing its 5-min ‘Flash’ electric car charging to Canada
summary:
BYD is preparing to bring its megawatt “Flash Charging” network, which can add about 250 miles (400 km) of range in 5 minutes, to Canada – the first confirmed deployment in North America.

The plan was revealed through a new BYD job posting in Toronto for a manager tasked with executing “BYD’s flash charging network expansion strategy and business growth across Canada.”

more…

title: EV sales just hit their best month since federal tax credits ended
summary:
New EV prices continued to slide in May, even as sales climbed to their strongest level since the Trump administration killed federal EV tax credits last fall.

more…

title: BYD’s new plug-in Super hatch packs 65 miles of EV range, promises to slash fuel costs
summary:
The Dolphin G DM-i is BYD’s first car specifically designed for Europe. With up to 65 miles of pure electric range and 646 miles combined, BYD said the plug-in hatch will redefine the segment as an affordable, efficient alternative.

more…

title: Kia EV2 delivers 105% of its official range estimate in the real world, ranking #1 in class
summary:
It may be Kia’s smallest electric vehicle, but the EV2 is a “big” deal. Kia’s electric SUV beat its official range estimate in a recent real-world test, outperforming all others in its class.

more…

title: Ford’s $30,000 EV pickup was just spotted in the wild for the first time
summary:
Just days after Ford said you might see one testing in public, the low-cost EV pickup was caught driving in Long Beach, and it’s even smaller than expected.

more…

title: Anker’s massive SOLIX Early Prime Day power station deals + exclusive bonus savings from $129, Ride1Up Portola e-bike, more
summary:
Our midweek Green Deals are following the trend of the last two days in terms of power station deals, with Anker’s SOLIX Early Prime Day Sale now in motion alongside a 48-hour flash sale offering a massive lineup of deals with many FREE gifts and our exclusive bonus savings code – all starting from $129. From there, Ride1Up is offering a clearance sale on its Portola Compact Folding e-bike starting from its second-lowest $895 price. We also spotted the second-ever deal on Worx’s 20V 5.75 GPM Water Transfer Pump with a 4.0Ah battery at a $130 low, the best price of 2026 on Greenworks’ 60V 18-inch chainsaw, and more waiting for you below. Don’t forget, we also have hangover deals collected down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Jackery Early Prime Day Sale with up to 65% power station discounts + up to 10% bonus savings, that sale’s flash event that ends tonight, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: BYD is deploying 2.4x more charging power per month than Tesla
summary:
BYD is building a charging network that makes Tesla’s Supercharger look slow — and it’s deploying it at a pace that could see it surpass Tesla’s global network within two to three years.

The Chinese automaker has already deployed over 5,700 Flash Charging stations in China in just a few months and just opened its first overseas stations in Europe. The chargers deliver up to 1,500 kW of power — three times what Tesla’s latest V4 Superchargers can do.

more…

title: BYD outsold Ford, and now it thinks it can catch Toyota as the world’s No. 1 automaker
summary:
BYD is already the world’s largest EV maker, but the company’s CEO is confident it will become the world’s leading global automaker within the next five years.

more…

title: BYD threatens to sue Trump administration over Pentagon military list
summary:
BYD is threatening to sue the US government after the Pentagon added the world’s largest EV maker to its list of “Chinese military companies” this week.

Stella Li, BYD’s executive vice-president, said the company will use every legal “weapon” at its disposal to fight the designation, which she called a false claim aimed at hobbling BYD because of its international success.

more…

title: Mercedes’ Smaller-Battery GLC EV Doesn’t Have A Range Problem
summary: Mercedes added two lower-priced electric GLC variants, and the rear-drive version still claims up to 404 miles of WLTP range.

title: Lucid's Top Engineer Departs The EV Startup As New CEO Takes Over
summary: Emad Dlala, Lucid's senior vice president of engineering and software, has left the company, TechCrunch reports.

title: Tesla Apparently Won't Let Cybertruck Buyers Transfer FSD Without Spending Another $20k
summary: Buyers of Tesla's cheapest Cybertruck trim ordered with the intention of transferring FSD—now they can't.

title: State-Backed Mexican EV Startup Unveils $8,600 EV With 6 Seats
summary: The Olinia Uno seats six, can take a wheelchair, and is made, designed, and developed entirely in Mexico.

title: Meet The New(ish) Cheap EV Hitting The U.S. This Summer
summary: The Mitsubishi Eclipse Sportback is based on the $30,000 Nissan Leaf and hits dealerships later this year.

title: BMW’s Electric M3 Doesn’t Imitate A V8. It Does Something Cooler
summary: The goal isn’t to copy a V8, V10 or straight-six, but to give drivers the speed and acceleration cues that EVs usually lack.

title: Teslas Are More Likely To Hit 250,000 Miles Than Almost Every Car Brand
summary: Think electric cars don't last? Very few companies beat Tesla in this study of the longest lasting car brands.

title: The Lucid Gravity's Latest Update Brings Hands-Free Driving And Google Maps Information
summary: Lucid's three-row SUV just got some big feature upgrades thanks to a software update.

title: This Used Audi E-Tron Lost A Fortune. Its Battery Held Up Much Better
summary: Once priced at nearly €110,000, this now six-year-old e-tron 55 sold used for €36,000 and it still has most of its original range left.

title: The 2027 Cadillac Lyriq Is Keeping A Big Feature All Other GM EVs Have Already Lost
summary: The updated luxury electric crossover is also getting a Tesla-style charge port and a small price bump.
